title: Infinite URL Spaces, Calendars, Facets, Sessions, and Budget Defense · Contract Lens: Query Cardinality Budget
summary: Calendar next links, faceted queries, session IDs, sort permutations, soft errors, and generated paths consume the crawl while each URL looks valid. Predict authorization, URL identity, runtime admission, Promise ownership, resource bounds, accessibility impact, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to contain calendar, facet, session, template, soft-error, and query-cardinality traps with stop evidence; owner authorization and revocation must remain separate from robots and legal-review claims; then change one owner permission, seed, URL, robots, sitemap, redirect, DNS, media, or encoding decision and reconcile provenance. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Budget distinct keys, values, combinations, orderings, and per-template pages with stakeholder-approved exceptions.

For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Query Cardinality Budget model · Theory Query Ca…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: An overall page cap makes query explosion harmless and explainable.
